title: [APIv2] The container cannot be started through the API
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

The container cannot be started through the API, return 500


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a container using the CLI or API

2. Error starting container using API 

```
curl --location --request POST 'http://xxxx:9999/v1.0.0/containers/a9706da650a87167900402b46f9c96ba1b9a8a0c4f942885f6b0fa7c8cae08be/start'
```

3. Starting the same container with CLI succeeded

**Describe the results you received:**

```
{
    "cause": "user: unknown userid 0",
    "message": "unable to resolve HOME directory: user: unknown userid 0",
    "response": 500
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7-dirty
Built:        Thu Jan  1 08:00:01 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: I can't reproduce. Did you compile Podman on your own or did you use a PPA?
I can run it if I compile it myself, but not with binary packages generated by cirrus
Which package are you referring to? Can you share a link?

Thanks!

Those binaries are meant for testing only but we don't officially support them (see https://github.com/containers/podman#static-binary-builds). There are certain issues with static go binaries that force us to only support dynamic ones.
